readings.summary: Despite rejection, Isaiah does not abandon his call.

readings.citation:
Ps 22:8-9, 17-18, 19-20, 23-24. R. v.2a
My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?
readings.antiphon:
Ps 22:8-9, 17-18, 19-20, 23-24. R. v.2a
My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?

readings.summary: Jesus made himself lowly and was exalted.

Christ became obedient to the point of death,
even death on a cross.
because of this, God greatly exalted him
and bestowed on him the name which is above every name.
The Passion of our Lord Jesus Christ.

antiphons.text:
Six days before the Passover,
when the Lord came into the city of Jerusalem,
the children ran to meet him;
in their hands they carried palm branches
and with a loud voice cried out:
*Hosanna in the highest!

Blessed are you, who have come in your abundant mercy!
O gates, lift high your heads; grow higher, ancient doors.
Let him enter, the king of glory! Who is this king of glory?
He, the Lord of hosts, he is the king of glory.
*Hosanna in the highest!

Blessed are you, who have come in your abundant mercy!

Father, if this chalice cannot pass without my drinking it,
your will be done.

prelude.title: No Prelude music.
prelude.composer: Reading on the Church Steps followed by

responsorialPsalm.psalm: Ps 22 My God... why have you abandoned me?
responsorialPsalm.setting: The Lyric Psalter • Tony Alonso & Marty Haugen

gospelAcclamation.title: Mass of Joy & Peace Lenten G.A. (Refrain)
gospelAcclamation.composer: The Lyric Gospel Acclamations • Tony Alonso
gospelAcclamation.verse:
Christ became obedient to the point of death, even death
on a cross. because of this, God greatly exalted him and bestowed on him the name which is above every name.

eucharisticAcclamations.composer: Marty Haugen • Arr. by Lockwood & Phan

fractionRite.title: Lamb of God (Mass of Creation)
fractionRite.composer: Marty Haugen • Arr. by Lockwood & Phan

communionSongs.title: Crux Fidelis

communionSongs.title: We Remember
communionSongs.description: "...and still we celebrate for you are with us here..."

communionSongs.description: "...but if it die, it will yield a rich harvest"
